checks.a_analysis: The output contains the main heading, the primary contact information, and the 'CJLEADS' text found at the bottom of the page. No fabricated paragraphs or sentences were found in the output. The document title is noted as AI-generated in the metadata, which is an acceptable change.

The contact information in the output (CJLEADS, 3514 Bush Street, Raleigh, NC 27609, 919-754-6949, cjleadshelp@nc.gov) matches the source document exactly. There are no fabricated numbers, emails, or addresses.

The single page of the source document is represented. Although significant content within the main section is missing (the flowchart steps), the section itself is initialized with its heading and some footer/contact text. The content omission is evaluated under check C5.
The source document does not contain any formal tables; it contains a flowchart which has been captured as an image element.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The core content of the document—the text contained within the seven steps of the onboarding flowchart—is entirely missing from the text elements of the JSON. While the flowchart is included as an image with a summary alt-text, the specific textual instructions (e.g., the 5 sub-steps in Step 2, the NCID organization check in Step 3, and training details in Steps 5-7) are not captured. This represents a loss of more than 25% of the document's total textual information.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source contains detailed steps in a flowchart: '1. Identifies & provides Signatory Authority...', 'Is requesting Agency an established NCID Organization?', 'CJLEADS Trains User Admins... 1. NCID Administration 2. CJLEADS User Admin', etc.
checks.b_evidence_output: The text content within the sections array only contains the main heading, contact block, and 'CJLEADS' footer. It entirely omits the procedural text from the flowchart.
checks.b_evidence_location: Main section 'How Does My Agency Get CJLEADS?', page 1

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text that was successfully extracted (heading, contact info) matches the source document precisely without any wording changes that alter meaning.

checks.a_analysis: A thorough review of the output JSON against the source PDF shows no instances of duplicated content. Each text block present in the output, such as the title, contact information, and footer text, appears only once, corresponding to its single appearance in the original document.

The main document title 'How Does My Agency Get CJLEADS?' is correctly captured as a Level 1 heading (H1). No other headings are defined in the output. While the flowchart contains logical sections (Step 1, Step 2, etc.), these are contained within a single graphic and their omission as separate text headings is not a break in existing hierarchy, but a flat structure which is acceptable for this document layout.
The source document does not contain any tables; therefore, there is no corrupted table structure in the output.

The reading order in the output JSON follows a logical top-to-bottom flow: Title, Primary Contact information (from the left sidebar), Footer text (from the bottom right), and then the main flowchart graphic. This sequence is a reasonable linearization of the document's spatial layout for accessibility and does not disrupt the intended meaning of the information.

The source document contains several numbered lists within the flowchart boxes (Step 2 has 5 items, Step 5 has 2 items, and other boxes have 2 items each). The output JSON completely fails to identify or convert any of these as structural list elements. The entire content of the flowchart, including these lists, has been relegated to an image alt-text summary, resulting in a loss of the document's list structure.
checks.b_evidence_source: Step 2: 'Requesting Agency: 1. Identifies & provides Signatory Authority 2. Identifies internal points of contact 3. Identifies authorized security roles 4. Signs License and Usage Agreement 5. Files signed documents with CJLEADS Team'
checks.b_evidence_output: The JSON contains no 'list' elements in the sections/elements array.
checks.b_evidence_location: Page 1, 'Execution of License and Usage Agreement' box

checks.a_analysis: The contact information section in the source PDF includes a phone number (919-754-6949) and an email address (cjleadshelp@nc.gov). In the converted output, these are represented as plain text within a paragraph and are not functional links. This is a failure to preserve the interactive nature of contact data clearly visible in the original document.
checks.b_evidence_output: "text": "Primary Contact: CJLEADS 3514 Bush Street Raleigh, NC 27609 919-754-6949 cjleadshelp@nc.gov"

checks.a_analysis: The source PDF features a flowchart where a 'Primary Contact' information box is positioned at the bottom-left of the page and is visually associated with 'Step 1' (Kickoff Event) via a connecting line. In the rendered HTML, this contact information block has been extracted and moved to the top of the document, preceding the flowchart. Consequently, the flowchart (rendered as an image) now contains a dangling line pointing to an empty white space where the contact box was originally located. This significantly alters the visual organization and breaks the spatial relationship between the contact info and the kickoff step.
checks.b_evidence_source: The 'Primary Contact' red text box is located at the bottom left of the page, below Step 2 and connected via a line to Step 1.
checks.b_evidence_output: The contact information is placed as plain text at the top of the document (below the main heading), and the flowchart image below it shows a line pointing to empty space where the box was originally situated.
checks.b_evidence_location: Page 1, bottom-left quadrant vs. top of HTML body.

checks.a_analysis: The source document does not contain any traditional data tables (rows and columns). The boxes in the flowchart are part of a diagram, not a table element.

The primary meaningful image in the document is the flowchart diagram. This is successfully rendered as an image in the HTML output, including the agency logos and the flowchart structure itself. While part of the content (the contact box) was removed from the diagram image and moved elsewhere, the image itself is present and functional.

While the document title 'How Does My Agency Get CJLEADS?' appears as a heading at the top and again as text at the bottom, this is common in automated conversions to provide text-based access to content also trapped inside an image. It does not constitute a duplicated content block (like a paragraph or table) that would confuse the reader or clutter the visual flow significantly beyond standard accessible design practices.
